company.deckText:
Gridwise Carbon Pitch Deck

Sector: ClimateTech / Carbon Accounting / B2B SaaS

Stage: pre-seed

Geography: UK / Europe / Worldwide

Raise amount: GBP 700,000

Traction: GBP 5k MRR, 15 pilot customers

Problem: Target customers waste time and money because existing workflows are fragmented, manual, and hard to measure.

Solution: Carbon accounting and supplier emissions tracking for SMEs.

Business model: B2B SaaS subscription with implementation support.

Team: Founder-led team with product, commercial, and domain experience; hiring plans focus on engineering, sales, and customer success.

Go-to-market: Start in the UK, expand into Europe, and selectively target worldwide investors and customers where the thesis matches.

Financial assumptions: current monthly revenue 5000, burn 39000, cash 150000, gross margin 76%.

Investor ask: Raising GBP 700,000 to fund the next 18 months, prove repeatable acquisition, and reach the milestones in the financial model.

Use of funds: product development, customer acquisition, compliance or data where relevant, and core hires.

deckSignals.summary: The pitch deck presents a pre-seed ClimateTech B2B SaaS startup focused on carbon accounting for SMEs. It outlines the problem related to fragmented, manual workflows causing inefficiency, proposes a SaaS solution, provides limited traction data (5k MRR, 15 pilots), details the team and go-to-market plans, and specifies a £700k raise with financial assumptions. However, it lacks detailed market sizing, detailed competitive landscape, customer acquisition strategy, and product differentiation. categoryScores.feedback: The problem is described as inefficient, fragmented workflows causing waste, which is clear but generic. More specifics on customer pain points or case examples would strengthen understanding.

The solution is defined as carbon accounting and supplier emissions tracking SaaS with implementation support, fitting the problem. However, feature details, product uniqueness, and differentiation from competitors are not provided.
The deck does not provide any quantification or credible estimates of the total addressable market or serviceable market. This is a notable omission for investor assessment.
The B2B SaaS subscription model with implementation support is stated clearly. Pricing, sales cycle, or customer acquisition cost details are missing, limiting evaluation.
The deck reports £5k MRR and 15 pilot customers which indicates initial validation. No growth metrics or revenue growth rates are provided.
Founder-led team with product, commercial, and domain experience is mentioned. Hiring plans are outlined but details on team members’ backgrounds, skills, or relevant achievements are absent.
The amount to raise (£700k) and use of funds are stated, along with current financials and runway assumption (18 months). However, detailed financial projections or milestones are not exhibited.
The deck logically connects problem, solution, market, traction, team, and raise but lacks a compelling story or differentiation that engages investors beyond basic facts.

financialSignals.notes: Monthly revenue, burn, cash, runway and raise amount are stated. Customer count from pilots included. Team size and growth metrics are missing.

strengths: Initial traction with pilot customers and revenue
Detailed capital raise amount and planned use of funds
weaknesses: No market size quantification or analysis
No demonstration of product differentiation or competitive edge
Lack of detailed financial projections or growth metrics
priorityActions: Build and present detailed financial projections linked to milestones
fundraisingRisks: Unclear market size risks underestimating opportunity
Limited traction may not convince investors
Lack of detailed team information reduces confidence
No clear competitive differentiation risks losing market share

rawLeads.company_description:
Almaz Capital was founded in 2008 to invest in early stage startups in the CIS (commonwealth of independent states) and CEE (central and eastern Europe) region with the backing of Cisco Systems and the EBRD (European Bank for Reconstruction and Development). Almaz Capital set out to invest and build a bridge between investee companies from the newly independent countries of the former Soviet Union to resources and connections in Silicon Valley. With that intention the firm opened its first offices in Silicon Valley and Moscow. During that period Almaz supported many startups with origins in Russia, such as QIK (acquired by Skype), Yandex (NASDAQ:YNDX) or Parallels (acquired by Corel).

In March 2014, after Crimea was annexed by Russia, Almaz Capital stopped investing in companies with any ties to Russia. In 2017 Almaz moved its European operations to Berlin and focus shifted from the countries of the CIS to the CEE region (central and eastern Europe). Following the crackdowns in Minsk in 2020 Almaz stopped investing in Belarus, another former Soviet state.

We are helping our Ukraine-based startups survive the war ravaging their country. Not only are we one of the largest investors in Ukraine over the last 5 years, but we are also a long-time supporter actively involved at the governance level at the UVCA (Ukrainian Venture Capital Association). Almaz Capital invested over $50m in over 15 companies with Ukrainian founders or teams including Adjoy, Arcycle, Content Analytics, DMarket, Hover, Jelastic, Mobalytics, Maketime, Petcube, Speedsize, Starwind, and 3Dlook . Startup teams have shown amazing resiliency and despite the war Ukraine remains one of our favorite investment destinations.

Raised in Houston, I moved to San Francisco as a freshly minted computer engineering graduate in 1999 to experience the tail-end of the first internet revolution. Living through and benefiting from the next wave of cloud adoption starting in the mid-2000s, I recognized in 2014 that another transition was underway, one where AI (artificial intelligence) is eating the same SaaS software which has slowly been consuming the world.

​Powered by this evolution, bold entrepreneurs around the world are pursuing new ideas, and it's a perfect time to find, nurture, and support the next generation of these breakthrough ventures. These ventures are brought to life through the hard work of innovators, who are looking to change the world by harnessing the power of Silicon Valley. Please reach out to us if you're interested to learn more about what makes us different and our approach to find, invest in, and develop the next generation of fund returning Silicon Valley unicorns.

Look forward to working with you to shape how humanity benefits from the broad adoption of artificial intelligence.

Sincerely,
Suman Talukdar
Founder & Managing Partner

rawLeads.company_description:
1.61 is known as the golden ratio — naturalists claim it is the most efficient ratio for growth to occur. Inspired by the ratio, we are building a community for startups to grow organically and efficiently.

Pablo Casilimas and Justis Mendez met at the University of Florida in 2016, where they recognized a significant gap in early-stage capital across Florida. Despite UF's robust talent pool—over 60,000 students and 13,000 engineers—resources for learning how to build startups and secure funding were scarce. Witnessing many talented peers leave the state to pursue their ventures elsewhere, they took action by hosting meetups, workshops, and roundtables to strengthen the local startup ecosystem and support founders.

By 2020, OneSixOne had become the hub for the largest startup events between Orlando and Atlanta. When the pandemic shifted the world to virtual, they adapted by launching a Zoom-based accelerator, helping 40+ startups raise over $13M in just two years, with one startup being acquired.

In February 2022, OneSixOne Ventures launched its flagship fund, focusing on early-stage startups in AI and cloud infrastructure, dual-use technology, and standout founders across industries.

Today, OneSixOne Ventures manages over $3M in assets with a portfolio of 15+ active companies. The firm continues to build communities through curated events, hosting 150+ gatherings across 8 cities and connecting over 6,000 founders, tech leaders, and investors.

2019 marked 20 years for me in Silicon Valley. Raised in Houston, I moved to San Francisco as a freshly minted computer engineering graduate in 1999 to experience the tail-end of the first internet revolution. Living through and benefiting from the next wave of cloud adoption starting in the mid-2000s, I recognized in 2014 that another transition was underway, one where AI (artificial intelligence) is eating the same SaaS software which has slowly been consuming the world.

​Powered by this evolution, bold entrepreneurs around the world are pursuing new ideas, and it's a perfect time to find, nurture, and support the next generation of these breakthrough ventures. These ventures are brought to life through the hard work of innovators, who are looking to change the world by harnessing the power of Silicon Valley. Please reach out to us if you're interested to learn more about what makes us different and our approach to find, invest in, and develop the next generation of fund returning Silicon Valley unicorns.

Look forward to working with you to shape how humanity benefits from the broad adoption of artificial intelligence.

Sincerely,
Suman Talukdar

The firm is led by AI pioneers, technologists, and seasoned operators. Leading with Expertise: The Quartus Edge
Quartus Capital Partners' leadership team boasts over 30 years of expertise in AI and technology, offering unique insights into high-growth potential sectors. As a firm, we don't just invest—we partner with companies, leveraging our deep expertise in growth and performance improvement to unlock their true potential.

Venture Growth Equity Strategy with a Performance Edge

Transforming Industries, Shaping the Future
At Quartus, we believe in the power of AI and technology to not only drive financial returns but to create lasting, positive change. We're investing in the future, in companies that are not just part of the AI revolution, but are leading it.

Ballistic Ventures is a new kind of venture capital firm, built by and for cybersecurity entrepreneurs and investors.

For over 35 years, our work in the field has granted us exclusive insights into the evolution of the cyber threat landscape. We've tracked its progression from the benign hacking of the 80s to the ruthless and insidious attacks on individuals and nations alike, which disrupt millions of lives daily.

As technological innovation continues at a blistering pace, every aspect of our physical lives will soon be accessible online. Without fail, nefarious acts will follow. There's no turning back. Adaptation is the only reasonable response.

Ballistic Ventures was formed out of a profound sense of urgency and moral responsibility to address the growing threats to society's digital infrastructure. 

We believe that the most important action we can take today is to empower the world's greatest cybersecurity entrepreneurs with our collective experience, network, and passion. Together, we can turn the tide at this crucial inflection point to ensure a safe, prosperous future for all generations to come.

For us, this mission is personal.
